findings.what: Two drills (L9-5d-1, L9-5d-2) anchor to exam sub-part '5d', but exam Q5 has only sub-parts a/b/c — there is no 5d, so these drills map to a non-existent exam sub-part.
findings.evidence: _map.json examQ 5 subParts = {a,b,c} only (a='difference between authentication and authorization...', b='key parts of XACML 3.0...', c='RBAC and ABAC approaches...'); _intent_9.txt lists Q5 (a)(b)(c) and nothing else. The drills' content (MVP sequencing / IAM governance body / vendor selection) is enterprise implementation strategy, not access control, and matches none of 5a/5b/5c.
findings.fix: Re-anchor L9-5d-1 and L9-5d-2 to a real sub-part or drop the sub-part claim. Their substance (incremental ROI, centralized governance body) most plausibly supports 5c (RBAC/ABAC governance challenges) at best, but it is implementation-strategy padding with no home in Q5's a/b/c; set anchorsSubPart to a valid letter (e.g. 'c') only if reframed around access-governance, otherwise treat as out-of-scope for the exam.

findings.what: Drill L9-5e-1 anchors to exam sub-part '5e', which does not exist (Q5 stops at c).
findings.evidence: _map.json examQ 5 subParts has no 'e'; _intent_9.txt only enumerates (a)(b)(c). Drill content is SSO/MFA/delegated admin for consumers (CIAM), which belongs conceptually to Q6 SSO, not to any Q5 sub-part.
findings.fix: Drop the '5e' anchor. If retained for cross-support, anchor to Q6 (SSO) as a crossRef-style support drill, or re-anchor to 5a ('how can authorization be implemented') only if reframed around fine-grained/scoped access rather than SSO.

findings.what: Three drills (L9-2a-1, L9-2b-1, L9-2b-2) anchor to Q2 a/b, but (i) Q2's crossRefs in _map.json are [6,8] — lecture 9 is NOT a declared cross-ref for Q2, and (ii) Q2's actual sub-parts a/b are about 'what is a digital identity' and 'identification vs identity proofing vs authentication', which these JML/HRMS/merger-split drills do not address.
findings.evidence: _map.json examQ 2 subParts: a='What is a digital identity? Discuss important concepts, terms and principles.', b='Explain the difference between identification, identity proofing and authentication.'; crossRefs=[6,8]. The drills instead cover Joiner-Mover-Leaver rationalization, HRMS data quality, and enterprise account split — none of which is digital-identity definition or identification/proofing/authentication. Source quotes themselves are accurate (IAM-Use-Case.txt PAGE 06 'rationalization of Joiner-Mover-Leaver', PAGE 05 'Improving data quality in source systems like HRMS', PAGE 07 'Migration of user privileges according to the new organization structure'), but they do not answer Q2a/b.
findings.fix: Either remove the Q2 anchor (this guest lecture's intent file only commits it to Q5) or, if kept, re-anchor to Q2's lifecycle theme via lecture 2's own framing — but Q2a/b text does not cover JML, so the cleanest fix is to label these as Q5-supporting (identity provisioning under access governance) rather than Q2.

coverageVerdict: All 12 drills are factually accurate vs source (every citation verified on the cited page). The lecture cross-supports Q5 (a/b/c) and the genuine Q5 material (provisioning/RBAC-ABAC-in-practice, centralized governance, PAM/tiering) is well covered; however the drill set's anchoring is broken: 3 drills point at non-existent sub-parts (5d x2, 5e) and 3 point at Q2 a/b whose actual text they do not answer (and lecture 9 is not a Q2 cross-ref). No factual WRONG findings; the exam-critical issue is mis-mapping, not content.
